stories.body: US Central Command said its latest wave of airstrikes targeted Iran’s coastal surveillance, air defense and IRGC sites in response to a Jordan attack that killed two American service members and left one missing, while Iranian media reported drone hits on US bases in Kuwait and claimed kamikaze assaults on Gulf infrastructure, prompting Kuwait and Bahrain to sound alarms and accuse Tehran of war crimes against civilian facilities; the US denies striking non-military targets even as Iran counts at least 50 dead and hundreds injured from American raids, and both sides accuse each other of violating a recently collapsed Strait of Hormuz ceasefire and the Islamabad memorandum of understanding on shipping security.
stories.headline: US and Iran trade escalating strikes amid rising casualties

stories.body: Russia launched one of its largest ballistic attacks on Kyiv, firing roughly 40 Iskander-M and Zircon missiles during a five-hour assault that killed at least one person, injured seven, and damaged homes, offices, a dormitory, and metro infrastructure, a day after Ukraine claimed drone strikes on logistics warehouses near Moscow and Tambov that killed eight; Kyiv residents endured multiple air raid sirens and collapsing ceilings while President Volodymyr Zelenskyy urged Western partners for more Patriot interceptors as Ukraine’s stockpiles thin amid continuing Russian salvos across the country.
stories.headline: Russian missile barrage batters Kyiv after Ukrainian drone strike near Moscow

stories.body: US Marshals detained Andrew and Tristan Tate in Miami under a sealed UK extradition warrant after Britain’s Crown Prosecution Service brought 59 total charges, including seven new rape counts, multiple trafficking and indecent image offenses, and allegations tied to incidents between 2010 and 2017; prosecutors say there are now seven alleged victims, while the brothers’ lawyer insists the cases are politically motivated even as UK authorities move to bring them back despite their ongoing legal battles in Romania.
stories.headline: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami on new UK rape and trafficking charges

stories.body: Hungarian President Tamás Sulyok signed the 17th constitutional amendment pushed through by Prime Minister Péter Magyar’s Tisza party, cutting short his mandate over a “serious loss of confidence” and triggering his departure at midnight Sunday; Sulyok condemned the move as a rupture of rule-of-law norms even as Tisza leaders say it dismantles Viktor Orbán’s entrenched influence, with Parliament Speaker Ágnes Forsthoffer set to serve as interim president until lawmakers, now constrained by new term limits and judicial retirement ages, select a successor.
stories.headline: Hungary’s president to resign after constitutional amendment ends his term

stories.body: Jens Spahn resigned as leader of Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s CDU parliamentary group after admitting he and his husband used a US surrogate despite Germany’s surrogacy ban, drawing accusations of hypocrisy because he previously defended the prohibition; Spahn said his family plans conflicted with political duties amid mounting criticism from CDU figures and opposition lawmakers, underscoring intra-party tensions as the CDU tries to recover in polls ahead of key eastern state elections.
stories.headline: Germany’s CDU parliamentary leader quits over US surrogacy revelation

stories.body: Maine Democrats began a compressed two-day process to pick a new Senate nominee after Graham Platner’s withdrawal, and progressive ex–state Senate president Troy Jackson used organized slates, union backing, and retail politics to win most of the first 319 county delegate slots—especially sweeping Portland’s Cumberland County—leaving rivals Nirav Shah and Shenna Bellows with limited paths before Sunday’s remaining selections and a 601-delegate convention that will determine who challenges Republican Senator Susan Collins.
stories.headline: Troy Jackson seizes delegate lead as Maine Democrats replace scandal-hit nominee

stories.body: Associated Press interviews and footage highlight protests and counter-demonstrations after ICE officer David Brouillette fatally shot Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, with the agent’s ex-wife describing a long history of mental health struggles and violence, fueling calls for accountability as bloodstained scenes and rallies across the state underscore tensions over immigration enforcement.
stories.headline: Scrutiny grows over ICE officer who killed Colombian man in Maine

stories.body: Thick smoke from Canadian and Minnesota wildfires shrouded Washington, New York City, and New Jersey, pushing air-quality alerts across much of the US and disrupting World Cup training at MetLife Stadium, but meteorologists say a passing storm front will clear most haze before Sunday’s Spain-Argentina final, even as President Donald Trump threatened tariffs on Canada and experts warned sensitive groups to keep monitoring particulate levels.
stories.headline: Wildfire smoke blankets Northeast ahead of World Cup final as storms promise relief

stories.body: The EU Court of Justice ruled Spain’s Catalan amnesty law is compatible with European law and can promote reconciliation, bolstering the government’s push to let exiled former Catalan president Carles Puigdemont return, yet opposition leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo and conservative media argue the measure remains a politically motivated trade for Junts votes and accuse Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of building his term on a lie despite earlier denials that amnesty was possible.
stories.headline: EU court backs Spain’s amnesty law but political fight reignites

stories.body: Madrid’s provincial court upheld Judge Juan Carlos Peinado’s plan to try Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife Begoña Gómez by popular jury for alleged embezzlement and influence peddling, trimming other counts but ordering a quick redo of procedural steps so the oral hearing—likely in spring 2027 barring an early election—can proceed, with prosecutors and a unified popular accusation recalibrating their filings as political sensitivities keep the case under intense scrutiny.
stories.headline: Begoña Gómez to face citizen jury on graft charges before next general election

stories.body: Investigators say Servinabar 2000 became the financial hub of an alleged kickback scheme linking former transport minister José Luis Ábalos, adviser Koldo García, and ex-PSOE organization chief Santos Cerdán, with Guardia Civil reports alleging Cerdán quietly bought 45% of the firm, spent about €323,000 over a decade via its accounts, negotiated luxury property deals, and had his spouse receive salary payments without working as Acciona-funded projects funneled cash through the company.
stories.headline: Probe zeroes in on Servinabar cash flows tied to PSOE heavyweight Santos Cerdán

stories.body: Organizers said about 140,000 people marched from Plaça Universitat to Passeig de Lluís Companys under the banner “All realities, one pride,” condemning LGBTI-phobic assaults after police arrested a man who allegedly threatened a couple with a glass bottle during Thursday’s opening events; Mayor Jaume Collboni and Catalan ministers backed Barcelona’s WorldPride 2030 bid while performers and dozens of groups turned the city center into a marathon of concerts and activism.
stories.headline: Pride Barcelona draws record crowd and renews WorldPride bid after homophobic attack

stories.body: A roughly eight-meter-wide sinkhole opened in a Sant Gervasi interior patio above ongoing Line 9 tunneling, collapsing part of a restaurant’s backyard and bathroom and forcing emergency crews to evacuate residents from eight buildings—covering 93 homes and more than 100 people—while authorities pumped over 300 cement trucks’ worth of concrete overnight before reassessing structural safety and arranging temporary lodging for displaced neighbors.
stories.headline: Sant Gervasi sinkhole linked to L9 works prompts mass evacuation

stories.body: Barcelona opened a new skate spot along Passeig de Garcia Faria featuring 14 concrete modules—nicknamed Bancalosa, Waves, So-ffa, and Flor—that double as seating and skating elements tied to the city’s World Capital of Architecture program, with a festive launch drawing riders and artists even as Diagonal Mar residents complained about noise, lack of consultation, and proximity to playgrounds in the densely populated beachfront corridor.
stories.headline: Architecture-led skate plaza debuts on Barcelona’s seafront amid neighbor concerns

stories.body: Sant Andreu’s Antoni Santiburcio Park reopened its long-idle water play area with limited 10 a.m.–8 p.m. hours, joining 17 other municipal splash zones aimed at offering free heat relief in neighborhoods far from beaches, but a newly installed installation on Avinguda de Mistral in Sant Antoni had to shut almost immediately after vandals broke a control button, forcing repairs even as residents call for more shaded play areas and faster rollouts elsewhere such as Plaça Carla Simó.
stories.headline: City expands splash-pad network as Sant Antoni site vandalized days after opening

stories.body: As polls from the Catalan government’s Centre d’Estudis d’Opinió show ultranationalist Aliança Catalana surging to potentially 23–25 seats—overtaking Junts’ projected 16–18—party secretary general Jordi Turull urged militants at a national council meeting to stay united, even as Sílvia Orriols’s formation courts Junts voters and local officials ahead of 2027 municipal races by recruiting former convergent figures and presenting itself as the hard-line alternative on independence and migration.
stories.headline: Junts tries to counter far-right Aliança Catalana’s momentum and defections
